description: Phase 33 high-signal adversarial PR scenarios for evaluating Pramaan without claiming correctness proof.

scenarios.name: Python checkout weakened assertion
scenarios.base_change: Base test asserts the exact discounted checkout total.
scenarios.head_change: Head test accepts any positive value, so the broken implementation passes ordinary CI.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: oracle_integrity failed receipt with weakened_assertion.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: Passing tests are untrustworthy because the assertion stopped checking the behavior named by the bug.
scenarios.replay_command: cargo run -p pramaan-cli -- oracle --base-repo examples/vulnerable-python-pr/base --head-repo examples/vulnerable-python-pr/weakened-test --out target/pramaan-demo/oracle

scenarios.name: Skipped regression test
scenarios.base_change: Base includes a failing regression test for the reported bug.
scenarios.head_change: Head marks the regression as skipped or xfail while changing production code.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: oracle_integrity failed receipt with skipped_or_ignored_test.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: A green build is not useful if the only relevant test was disabled.

scenarios.name: Invented import in generated fix
scenarios.base_change: The repository has no helper module for the generated import.
scenarios.head_change: The AI-authored patch imports an unavailable symbol.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: static_checks failed receipt with invented API/import risk.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: The code looks plausible but depends on an API that the repo cannot resolve.
scenarios.replay_command: cargo run -p pramaan-cli -- static-checks --repo examples/hallucinated-rust-pr --out target/pramaan-demo/hallucinated-rust

scenarios.name: Changed-line mutation survivor
scenarios.base_change: Base behavior has a narrow boundary condition.
scenarios.head_change: Head adds a broad test that misses an equivalent-looking changed-line mutant.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: mutation receipt with survived mutant and below-threshold risk if enough mutants survive.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: The PR added tests, but the changed behavior is still not pinned down.

scenarios.name: Unexpected differential divergence
scenarios.base_change: Base and head should agree outside the named bug scope.
scenarios.head_change: Head changes behavior for unrelated generated inputs.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: differential fuzz receipt with unexpected divergence.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: The fix may solve the issue while regressing untouched behavior.

scenarios.name: Snapshot and fixture drift
scenarios.base_change: Base fixture and snapshot encode the old expected order semantics.
scenarios.head_change: Head updates fixture/snapshot artifacts at the same time as code changes.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: oracle_integrity failed receipt with sensitive_artifact_changed.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: The expected output changed, so reviewers need to inspect the oracle itself.
scenarios.replay_command: cargo run -p pramaan-cli -- oracle --base-repo examples/snapshot-fixture-drift-pr/base --head-repo examples/snapshot-fixture-drift-pr/head --out target/pramaan-demo/snapshot-fixture-drift

scenarios.name: Input validation removed from API boundary
scenarios.base_change: Base rejects malformed user input before it reaches business logic.
scenarios.head_change: Head removes the validation branch to satisfy a narrow happy-path test.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: static security-sensitive diff plus missing property/fuzz validation evidence.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: The bug fix may work for one input while reopening invalid-input paths.

scenarios.name: Authorization guard weakened to ownership hint
scenarios.base_change: Base requires an explicit permission check before reading another user's record.
scenarios.head_change: Head replaces the permission check with a nullable owner-id comparison.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: security-sensitive diff warning and hard-gate under security-sensitive policy.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: Authorization regressions need stronger evidence than a passing CRUD test.

scenarios.name: Unsafe deserialization introduced for compatibility
scenarios.base_change: Base parses only JSON from trusted schema fields.
scenarios.head_change: Head uses pickle/yaml unsafe loading to accept legacy payloads.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: security-sensitive static signal plus required reviewer warning.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: A compatibility shortcut can create remote-code-execution risk even when tests pass.

scenarios.name: SQL sanitization removed by generated refactor
scenarios.base_change: Base uses parameterized queries or escaping at the SQL boundary.
scenarios.head_change: Head interpolates untrusted input into query text.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: static security-sensitive diff and property/fuzz warning for missing injection corpus.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: The generated code may pass fixture tests while creating an injection primitive.

scenarios.name: Crypto verification downgraded for flaky tests
scenarios.base_change: Base validates signatures, nonce freshness, or constant-time comparison.
scenarios.head_change: Head accepts unsigned tokens or replaces constant-time comparison with a normal string compare.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: security-sensitive diff escalated by strict policy.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: Crypto changes are high-risk even when ordinary tests are green.

scenarios.name: Secret exposure through helpful logging
scenarios.base_change: Base redacts token and password fields before logging.
scenarios.head_change: Head logs full request bodies to debug a generated failing test.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: static security-sensitive diff and redaction policy warning.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: Debuggability cannot trade off against leaking credentials in logs or bundles.

scenarios.name: GitHub workflow prompt injection in PR body
scenarios.base_change: Base PR metadata is ordinary issue text.
scenarios.head_change: Head PR body includes instructions to make reviewers or agents ignore failed checks.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: claim-scope receipt records R-093 workflow-injection signal.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: Untrusted PR text must not control verifier or reviewer behavior.

scenarios.name: Verifier config weakens required stages
scenarios.base_change: Base policy requires oracle and static stages.
scenarios.head_change: Head changes workflow/config to skip oracle or mutation on agent PRs.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: policy warning or hard gate for suspicious verifier-surface change.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: A PR must not improve its own confidence by disabling the verifier.

scenarios.name: Plugin emits unsigned trusted receipt
scenarios.base_change: Base verifier expects least-privilege plugin receipts.
scenarios.head_change: Malicious plugin receipt asks for manifest mutation and untrusted provenance.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: bundle verification rejects dangerous plugin permissions/provenance.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: The verifier plugin layer is part of the trust boundary and must be auditable.

scenarios.name: Receipt timestamp changes canonical digest
scenarios.base_change: Base receipt hashing separates event time from signing time.
scenarios.head_change: Head includes mutable signing-time data in the content hash.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: bundle verification rejects digest mismatch or nondeterministic canonicalization.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: Auditable bundles require deterministic hashes across replay.

scenarios.name: Redaction export leaks internal path
scenarios.base_change: Base bundle contains internal runner paths only in internal-full evidence.
scenarios.head_change: Public export preserves private path or host metadata in a receipt summary.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: redaction validation fails for public-demo export.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: A useful proof bundle still has to be safe to share outside the company.

scenarios.name: Claim scope omits changed public API
scenarios.base_change: Base public API is unchanged relative to the issue.
scenarios.head_change: Head changes a public export that is not mentioned in PR title or issue text.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: claim-scope receipt records changed public API mismatch.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: A fix may be technically valid but broader than reviewers intended to approve.

scenarios.name: Static checker disabled in config
scenarios.base_change: Base has strict type or lint settings.
scenarios.head_change: Head lowers strictness or disables a rule while adding generated code.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: static_checks receipt records relaxed configuration risk.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: The PR may only be green because the guardrail was lowered.

scenarios.name: Runtime fallback hides unsupported branch
scenarios.base_change: Base raises a visible error for unsupported input.
scenarios.head_change: Head silently returns a default value that makes tests pass.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: property/fuzz replay flags unexpected output or missing invariant coverage.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: Silent fallbacks can convert a known failure into corrupt downstream behavior.

scenarios.name: Network call added to pure function
scenarios.base_change: Base pure function is deterministic and offline.
scenarios.head_change: Head calls a network service to satisfy a test fixture.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: sandbox or static evidence records network-sensitive behavior and residual reproducibility risk.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: Verification has to surface hidden network dependence before it becomes CI drift.

scenarios.name: Fork PR tries to access secret-dependent path
scenarios.base_change: Base workflow avoids exposing secrets to forked PR code.
scenarios.head_change: Head adds a step that prints or branches on secret availability.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: workflow security signal and documentation warning for fork permission boundaries.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: CI isolation is part of the verifier's threat model, not an implementation detail.

scenarios.name: Benchmark overfit fixture branch
scenarios.base_change: Base handles the general class of inputs.
scenarios.head_change: Head special-cases only the published corpus value.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: mutation/property evidence should flag narrow behavior or leave R-100 corpus residual.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: Public demos cannot become the only thing the verifier checks.

scenarios.name: Flaky test converted to retry loop
scenarios.base_change: Base flaky failure exposes an async race.
scenarios.head_change: Head adds retries/sleeps without fixing the race.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: runtime drift and flaky-case quarantine warning in later calibration phases.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: Retries may reduce visible failures while leaving the race in production.

scenarios.name: Reviewer override hides repeated agent failure
scenarios.base_change: Base bundle records a warning and reviewer accepts the residual risk.
scenarios.head_change: Override reason is stored only in an ephemeral PR comment.
scenarios.pramaan_expected_finding: Phase 34 should persist override and trend evidence instead of losing reviewer context.
scenarios.reviewer_explanation: Humans can approve red evidence, but the decision must remain auditable.
